title: podocarpus oleifolius subsp. oleifolius
intro: Podocarpus oleifolius subsp. oleifolius, also known as the oil-leaved podocarp or Chinese plum yew, is a plant species in the coniferous family Podocarpaceae. It is traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been shown to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving coughs, and treating respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are typically used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions, although the exact methods and dosages may vary depending on the specific application.
